title: Throttling and limits
description: Distinguish you-agent-factory resource capacity, provider limits, queue pressure, and retry/limit behavior without inventing a global throttle guarantee.
openingSummary: Factory slowdowns and stops come from different pressure surfaces — configured capacity, provider quotas, queue backlog, and workstation retry limits — not one global throttle.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This page is the you-agent-factory reference for throttling and limits: how configured resource capacity, provider limits, queue pressure, and retry/limit behavior show up as distinct pressure surfaces when a factory run slows or stops. It is not a Model Atlas page, not a resources-only deep-dive, and not a fabricated global rate-limit SLA.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: Configured resource capacity, provider limits, queue pressure, and retry/limit (circuit-breaker) behavior are different mechanisms. Treat them as separate surfaces when diagnosing load — not as one undifferentiated throttle guarantee.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Use this page when a run feels constrained and you need to tell capacity, provider quota, queue backlog, and workstation limits apart. Ground each surface in current product behavior only; do not invent backoff schedules, numeric SLAs, or undocumented auto-throttles.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page publishes the throttling-and-limits documentation route and registry identity for you-agent-factory. It does not invent backoff schedules, numeric SLAs, or undocumented auto-throttles, and it is not a guarantee sheet for provider cloud quotas.
